title: We see Jesus
summary: “But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.”Heb 2:9  The greatest vision is the vision of faith, not sight. If the eyes of faith are closed, one is blind indeed. “And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire” (Matthew 18:9). One way of interpreting the words of the Lord is to think of the carnal flesh as having two eyes, while the spiritual person sees with just one eye – the eye that see Jesus. Consider how many people saw the Lord Jesus during his ministry and yet despised and reviled him! The Pharisees, Sadducees, Herodians, Herod and Pilate, all saw the Lord Jesus, but did not benefit from what their eyes beheld. On the other hand, thank God for people like Anna and Simeon! These were prophets who already saw the Lord Jesus through the eyes of the Holy Spirit. “Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:  For mine eyes have seen thy salvation” (Luke 2:29,30).
